scientific_integrity.detail: The legacy review kept this package on the proposal-design side of research support, not the result-reporting side.

practice_boundaries.detail: Practice boundaries were preserved because the outputs stayed within research-design support rather than executed-study claims.

methodological_ground.detail: The legacy review kept the package aligned with its named analysis library, data structure, or processing workflow.

code_usability.detail: This package is packaging-first and output-first, not code-first, so code usability is treated as not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: The archived deduction in functional suitability traces back to: Improve stress-case output rigor. Stress and boundary scenarios show weaker consistency

reliability.note: Related legacy finding for hypothesis-generation: Improve stress-case output rigor. Stress and boundary scenarios show weaker consistency

performance_context.note: Performance-context scoring suggests the package could compress some of its longer planning paths more cleanly.

human_usability.note: The legacy audit gave full marks to human usability for this package.

security.note: A small security deduction remained because safety and scope guardrails could still be stated more forcefully.

maintainability.note: The package remains maintainable, though the archived review saw modest room to simplify or stabilize its planning logic.
